short: When training a model on new data erases skills it had previously learned.
definition: Catastrophic forgetting is the tendency of a neural network to lose earlier knowledge when trained on new data, because the adjustments for the new task overwrite the internal values that stored the prior skills. It is a central challenge in continual learning and a practical concern when fine-tuning. Common fixes include mixing in old data, nudging the values to stay close to their previous settings, and methods like LoRA that train only a small set of added values.
